your_book:
Jaleel's book ended Friday, July 17 with a $21,545.83 account value and an unrealized loss of $212.16 (–0.98%), a broadly defensive session that had a handful of bright spots surrounded by widespread, if moderate, selling. The standout mover was Abbott Laboratories (ABT, 11.2% weight), which gained +1.87% to $2,416.32 — the book's largest dollar contributor on the day and, at +16.4% cumulative gain, one of its clearest wins. Adobe (ADBE, 17.3% weight, $3,716.90) also posted a rare up day at +0.82%, welcome news for the book's single biggest position but hardly enough to erase the –3.2% running loss. On the other side, NVIDIA (NVDA) dropped –2.21% to $1,323.48 — headline context being Apple reclaiming the world's largest-company title — while Uber (UBER, –2.13%) and Lululemon (LULU, –2.07%) also dragged. Coca-Cola (KO) was Friday's worst performer at –3.96%, hit by UK caffeine-ban clarity; at 0.8% weight the dollar damage is minimal, but the news adds a real-world headwind to an already low-conviction HOLD-QUAL name.

The drift problem continues to sharpen in two places. Rocket Lab (RKLB) sits at –26.1% cumulative with an AVOID verdict and a QARP of 44.6 — the position is $1,893.36, nearly 9% of the book, and price and thesis are not just diverging, they never converged under the current scoring. Boston Scientific (BSX) is rated STRONG BUY (QARP 82.7) but carries an elevated risk flag — guidance was slashed to 7–8.5% growth, management guided flat through Q3, and the next earnings print is July 29. The combination of a –8.0% cumulative loss and a hard guidance cut is exactly the kind of thesis erosion a capital-preservation book should not sit through passively. Lululemon (LULU) is in a similar pattern: –9.0% cumulative, guidance cut in June, five straight negative Americas comps, tariff margin pressure, and China stalled — yet it remains a BUY at 4.9% weight.

Watch list for next week: NOW reports July 22 — at 11.0% weight and a high multiple it is the book's macro-beta lightning rod; a soft print or cautious tone would hurt the whole growth sleeve. BSX on July 29 is the more urgent catalyst given the already-slashed guide. ADBE's narrative risk (AI-moat fear, CFO exit) means it stays on watch even though Friday's price action was constructive. And the GILD/NVS/NBIX/VRTX pharma cluster carries a live drug-pricing policy overhang that has a flag-review date of August 6.

attention.headline: AVOID-rated, –26% in the hole, nearly 9% of book — what is the exit plan?
attention.note: With a QARP of 44.6, a hard AVOID verdict, and a –26.1% cumulative loss consuming $1,893 of a capital-preservation portfolio, there is no thesis alignment here; continuing to hold requires an explicit, documented rationale or a trim decision.

attention.headline: Guidance slashed, earnings July 29 — thesis under active stress
attention.note: Management already cut FY26 growth to 7–8.5% and guided flat through Q3; with earnings in 12 days and a –8.0% cumulative loss, the STRONG BUY rating and the fundamental reality are pulling in opposite directions and the July 29 print will force the question.

attention.headline: Fifth straight negative Americas comp, guidance cut, tariff headwinds — drift widening
attention.note: At –9.0% cumulative with an elevated risk flag covering margin compression, stalled China growth, and a September 2 next catalyst, the gap between the BUY verdict and the operational reality deserves a hard look before that print.

attention.headline: –3.96% on UK caffeine-ban clarity — small weight but real fundamental read-through
attention.note: At 0.8% weight ($164) the position impact is negligible, but the UK policy headline adds a tangible demand headwind to a name already rated HOLD-QUAL with the book's lowest QARP (61.6); worth confirming the position still earns its seat.
